answer:
\boxed{C}

Sensitivity is defined as the proportion of **true positives** among all **actual cases** of the disease. Here, the test is the PSA level (>5 ng/mL), and the reference standard is the biopsy. To calculate sensitivity, you need the number of men with **positive test results** who **actually have prostate cancer** (true positives) and the total number of men with prostate cancer (true positives + false negatives). Since biopsies were only done on those with PSA >5 ng/mL, **Option C** provides the true positives (90 men with cancer) and the total cases tested (100 men biopsied), which is necessary to determine sensitivity.
